meaning: adj.赞成的,有利的,赞许的,良好的,讨人喜欢的,起促进作用的
examples: Some plants can even survive in favorable microhabitats above the snow line.
Favorable policies are in effect to encourage employees' professional development.
The book received highly favorable reviews.
Very, very few companies are in as favorable of a position to reward shareholders.
The meal from appetizers to desert was not only favorable but artfully prepared and flawlessly served.
